JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SANDRA EASTBURN RENEWED MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO ADD KIT COLE AS THE ALTER EGO OF KIT COLE INVESTMENT ADVISORY SERVICES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SATISFIED HER BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT COLE WAS AN ALTER EGO OF KCIAS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

SINCE A DECISION IS LIKELY TO BE ISSUED IN THE MARIN CASE IN THE NEAR FUTURE AND THAT DECISION MAY MOOT THE NEED FOR THIS MOTION AND/OR RESULT IN INFORMATION BEARING ON THIS MOTION, PRINCIPLES OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND DEFERENCE TO ANOTHER COURT OF THE SAME LEVEL WARRANT NOT RULING ON THE MERITS OF THIS MOTION AT THE PRESENT TIME. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Renewed Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Kit Cole As The Alter Ego Of Kit Cole Investment Advisory Services
Off Calendar Feb-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SANDRA EASTBURN renewed Motion to Amend Judgment to add Kit Cole as the Alter Ego of Kit Cole Investment Advisory Services. Appearances: Steven A. Dillick, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 399-8777 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Simona Agnolucci, Esq. and Gilbert Serota of Howard Rice (415) 434-1600 appearing for Third party Kit Cole and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Plaintiff has not satisfied her burden of demonstrating that Cole was an alter ego of KCIAS. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797

Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Kit Cole As Alter Ego; Motion To Amend;
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

PLAINTIFF SANDRA EASTBURN'S Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Kit Cole As Alter Ego; Motion To Amend; Denied without prejudice to re-calendaring the motion once a decision is issued in the Marin Superior Court action. Since a decision is likely to be issued in the Marin case in the near future and that decision may moot the need for this motion and/or result in information bearing on this motion, principles of judicial economy and deference to another court of the same level warrant not ruling on the merits of this motion at the present time. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 (301/HEK)
